tasks.spec: You are researching {{RESEARCH_QUESTION}} for {{DELIVERABLE — e.g. a technical guide}}. Use web search aggressively; prefer primary sources ({{PRIMARY_SOURCES — official docs, vendor pages, repos}}) over blog posts, and record the URL and access date for every claim. Work only inside your current working directory. Write ./report.md with sections: ANSWER (the current state, dated), EVIDENCE (claim → source URL → quote), UNCERTAIN (what you could not confirm and why). A claim without a cited source does not go in ANSWER.
tasks.check: test -s report.md && grep -q '^## ANSWER' report.md && grep -q '^## EVIDENCE' report.md && grep -qE 'https?://' report.md || { echo 'FAIL: report.md missing required sections or contains no source URLs'; exit 1; }
tasks.verified: the report has answer and evidence sections and cites source links

tasks.spec: You are building the EXECUTABLE PROOF that the core claim of this research holds: {{CLAIM_TO_PROVE}}. Work only inside your current working directory. Build {{PROOF_ARTIFACT — e.g. a script plus a self-contained test fixture that requires no real credentials or external accounts}} using {{CONSTRAINTS — e.g. Python 3 stdlib only}}. The proof must be runnable end-to-end by the check command below with no manual steps, print PROOF OK on success, and print a specific failing step on failure. Also write ./proof.md explaining what the proof demonstrates and its limits.
tasks.check: {{PROOF_COMMAND — e.g. python3 proof.py 2>&1 | tail -20}} && test -s proof.md || { echo 'FAIL: proof did not execute successfully or proof.md missing'; exit 1; }
tasks.verified: the proof actually executed end-to-end and reported success
